data.title: 'In the near term': Four words has Wall Street rebounding more than 1%
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday bounced back strongly from its stunning reversal in the previous session, largely led by a rebound in December interest rate cut odds thanks to some dovish comments from New York Fed President John Williams.

All three major averages jumped above 1% each. The benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) was last +1.3%, the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.2%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +1.5%. The Nasdaq on Thursday had initially gained more than 2%, only to end the day sliding more than 2%.  

Four words did the trick on Friday.

"I view monetary policy as being modestly restrictive, although somewhat less so than before our recent actions. Therefore, I still see room for a further adjustment in the near term to the target range for the federal funds rate to move the stance of policy closer to the range of neutral, thereby maintaining the balance between the achievement of our two goals," Williams said in prepared remarks on Friday at a Central Bank of Chile conference.

December rate cut odds surged to 71.5%, according to the CME FedWatch tool, from 39.1% a day ago. 

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the three major averages: (DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QLD), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Novamax rises after a seven-day slide
data.description:
Novavax (NVAX) rose 4.98% to $6.53 in afternoon trading on Friday, snapping a seven-session losing streak, with notable drops of 3.38% and 3.50% on November 18 and 19.

Novavax now expects to reach profitability in 2028, with the timing tied to upcoming vaccine launches under its Sanofi partnership.

The company also shared updated revenue outlook, noting higher guidance for next year even as its latest results showed a wider quarterly loss. It is hoping that the upcoming COVID–flu combination shot will help strengthen its outlook, but demand for its current COVID vaccine has stayed soft.

At the same time, one of its major shareholders has been pressing for broader changes. The investor has raised concerns about the slow uptake of the company’s vaccine and said Novavax has not been able to turn its science into stronger commercial results, adding pressure on the board to review its options.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating, NVAX is rated Hold with a score of 2.81 out of 5, highlighted by A+ in valuation, B- in profitability, but weaker growth with a C- rating, D+ in momentum, and F in revisions.

SA analysts also recommend holding the stock.

While on the Wall Street, five of nine analysts rate the shares Strong Buy, while one advises Hold and three rate Sell or lower.

Despite today’s rebound, shares remain down by 18.78% YTD and have lost around 24% in the past month.

data.title: Home Depot set to snap seven straight sessions of losses
data.description:
Home Depot (HD) shares set to snap seven straight sessions of losses, as the stock gained more than 3.5% during afternoon trading on Friday.

However, the firm’s shares slipped about 11.5% in the preceding seven sessions.

HD is down 11.7% over the past month. The stock closed 0.63% lower on Thursday at $332.38.

Earlier this week, the company reported mixed Q3 earnings as its Non-GAAP EPS of $3.74 missed the consensus estimate of $3.83 by $0.09, while its revenue increased 2.8% Y/Y to $41.35 billion.

Seeking Alpha analyst YR Research highlighted that Home Depot (HD) missed estimates once again, as a highly anticipated market recovery continues to be pushed out.

"Perhaps the guidance cut will finally be the driver that realigns market expectations with a pressured consumer reality, as Home Depot now expects comparable sales to grow less than 1%," advised YR Research.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, HD has a Hold rating with a score of 3.08 out of 5. The company received an A+ for profitability and a D- for valuation and growth factors.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 23 out of 37 analysts rated HD a Buy and above, 14 analysts gave the stock a Hold recommendation, and no one rated it a Sell or below.

Overall, the stock has declined more than 13% so far this year, compared to a nearly 12% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

data.title: Google exec says compute needs to be doubled every 6 months to meet demand: report
data.description:
An executive working on artificial intelligence infrastructure at Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) recently told employees the company needs to double its compute capacity every six months to meet demand, CNBC reported.

Amin Vahdat, a vice president at Google Cloud, gave a presentation to employees that included a slide on compute capacity, which read “Now we must double every 6 months.... the next 1000x in 4–5 years,” the news outlet reported, citing the presentation. The comments were made at a meeting on Nov. 6. 

“The competition in AI infrastructure is the most critical and also the most expensive part of the AI race,” Vahdat said during the meeting.

The search giant needs to “be able to deliver 1,000 times more capability, compute, storage networking for essentially the same cost and increasingly, the same power, the same energy level,” Vahdat added. “It won’t be easy, but through collaboration and co-design, we’re going to get there.”

A Google spokesperson told Seeking Alpha that the claim was “misleading.” 

"Amin was not talking about a capital buildout of anything approaching the magnitude suggested”" the spokesperson said via email. “In reality, he simply noted that demand for AI services means we are being asked to provide significantly more computing capacity, which we are driving through efficiency across hardware, software, and model optimizations, in addition to new investments. For example, our new Ironwood pods offer a 10x peak performance improvement than their predecessors released just a year ago.”  

Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai and CFO Anat Ashkenazi were also at the meeting and took questions.

One of the questions Pichai took from an employee was about the potential bursting of the AI bubble. Pichai acknowledged the fact that the question is now in the “zeitgeist,” but reiterated his stance that the risk of underinvesting is high. 

“I think it’s always difficult during these moments because the risk of underinvesting is pretty high,” Pichai said, according to CNBC. “I actually think for how extraordinary the cloud numbers were, those numbers would have been much better if we had more compute.” 

The report comes one day after tech giant Nvidia (NVDA) reported better-than-expected results and guidance. Shares initially rose after the figures were released, and management had a response for several investor concerns. However, the gains evaporated on Thursday amid continued worries about the bursting of the AI bubble.

Alphabet is one of Nvidia's customers and also uses its own tensor processing units for various applications and services.

(This story has been updated to reflect Google's response.)

data.title: International Land Alliance secures up to $50M in institutional financing
data.description:
• International Land Alliance (ILAL) on Friday said it has secured an up to $50 million financing facility from an institutional investor.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds to acquire 300 acres of land and structures located adjacent to the its Rancho Costa Verde development in San Felipe, Mexico.
  • Simultaneously, ILAL entered into an agreement to provide real estate services for the properties of such investor.
  • The company intends to use a portion of the initial proceeds from the financing facility to support acquisitions, joint ventures, project expansions, and serve as a strategic reserve to support long-term growth opportunities.
  • ILAL is +6.32% to $0.1699.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Moog projects 9% sales growth and $10 EPS for FY26 as backlog hits $3B record
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Moog Inc. (MOG.A) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Patrick Roche, President and CEO, highlighted record-breaking fourth quarter and full-year results, stating, “We achieved record results. This performance capped an outstanding full year in which we achieved strong growth, continued margin expansion and improved free cash flow.” Roche emphasized operational execution, noting “orders, backlog and sales, which are respectively up 36%, 20% and 7% relative to prior year.” He outlined that the company’s 12-month backlog reached an all-time high of $3 billion, and fourth quarter sales surpassed $1 billion for the first time.
  • Roche detailed continued strength in the Defense market, stating, “We’re experiencing a secular increase in Defense spending within the U.S., NATO Nations and Indo-Pacific allies, which will continue for the foreseeable future.” He also called out contract wins in Australia and Europe, and progress in the collaborative combat aircraft segment.
  • Roche explained that Moog’s industrial business is seeing “steady growth in the medical end market and outsized growth in data center cooling,” with doubled volume for data center cooling pumps in the last nine months.
  • Roche reported, “We have embedded 80/20 into approximately 80% of our businesses by revenue,” with simplification initiatives delivering margin benefits comparable to those from pricing and volume growth. He highlighted that, “From fiscal '22 to fiscal '25, while controlling headcount increases to just 4% and reducing our factory space by 8%, we've driven a 27% increase in sales.”
  • Looking ahead, Roche stated, “Sales will be up 9% year-over-year, and adjusted operating margin, exclusive of tariffs in both years will be up 70 basis points. FY '26 adjusted EPS will be up 15% and free cash flow will strengthen to 60%.”
  • Jennifer Walter, Executive VP & CFO, provided an update on a correction of an accounting error, stating, “The net impact of these changes increases our net earnings per share by $0.13 in fiscal year '23, $0.05 in fiscal year '24 and $0.06 in the first 9 months of fiscal year '25.”
  • Walter summarized, “Sales for FY '25 were $3.9 billion. This represents a 7% increase over FY '24. Our Aerospace and Defense segments drove this growth.” She reported that adjusted earnings per share for FY '25 were $8.69, up 11%.

Outlook

  • Moog projects FY '26 sales of $4.2 billion, representing a 9% increase compared to FY '25, with the largest growth in Aerospace and Defense and a modest increase in Industrial.
  • Commercial Aircraft sales are projected to grow 15% to $1.0 billion, Space and Defense to increase 11% to $1.2 billion, and Military Aircraft to grow 7% to $1.0 billion. Industrial sales are projected to increase 3% to $1.0 billion.
  • Adjusted operating margin is forecast to be 13.4% for FY '26, up 40 basis points over FY '25. Excluding tariffs, operating margin would be 14.2%.
  • FY '26 earnings per share are projected to be $10 plus or minus $0.20, up 15% over FY '25 adjusted EPS. First quarter FY '26 EPS is forecast at $2.20, plus or minus $0.10.
  • Free cash flow conversion is projected to improve to 60% in FY '26.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter sales exceeded $1 billion, a 14% increase over the prior year’s fourth quarter.
  • Commercial Aircraft sales of $252 million rose 27% year-over-year, Space and Defense sales were $307 million (up 17%), Military Aircraft sales were $236 million (up 10%), and Industrial sales reached $253 million (up 5%).
  • Adjusted operating margin for the quarter was 13.7%, up 20 basis points from the previous year’s quarter.
  • Adjusted EPS for the quarter was $2.56, up 19% year-over-year.
  • Free cash flow generation was about $200 million for the quarter.

Q&A

  • Jonathan Tanwanteng, CJS Securities, Inc.: Asked about free cash flow phasing for the next year and underlying improvement initiatives. Jennifer Walter responded that FY '26 is forecasted at 60% free cash flow conversion, driven by working capital initiatives in Commercial Aircraft and ongoing destocking efforts. Patrick Roche highlighted structural changes in supplier relationships and payment terms as long-term cash flow enhancements.
  • Tanwanteng inquired about Commercial Aircraft margin pressures. Walter confirmed margin pressure from tariffs and a negative mix impact due to aftermarket being a smaller share.
  • Michael Ciarmoli, Truist Securities, Inc.: Asked for targets on working capital as a percentage of revenue and bridging items to cash in '26. Walter noted no long-term target for '26 yet but reaffirmed the 75% to 100% free cash flow conversion goal. CapEx is projected to stay near $160 million. Roche and Walter discussed project timelines and returns, indicating benefits will materialize over two to three years.
  • George Bancroft, Gabelli ETFs Trust: Asked about growth platforms and potential M&A. Roche noted ongoing interest in acquisitions, especially in Defense, and expansion efforts in Australia and Europe.
  • Ciarmoli asked why tariffs hit Aerospace more than Industrial. Roche attributed it to the global supply chain and certain contracts in Commercial Aircraft.
  • Ciarmoli explored Commercial Aircraft growth composition; Jennifer Walter said growth is mainly in OE, with some narrow-body volume increases.
  • Confidence in meeting the 60% free cash flow conversion target was confirmed by Walter: “Yes, we're confident in our projection that we've got out for this year.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were generally positive, with questions focusing on execution confidence, cash flow improvement, and margin pressure, but also pressing for details about structural changes and mix impacts.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, emphasizing operational strength and guidance visibility. Walter stated, “Yes, we're confident in our projection for next year.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment from both analysts and management remains positive, with management’s tone remaining consistent in confidence but more detailed in addressing structural and operational enhancements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for FY '26 is more specific, projecting 9% sales growth, increased operating margin, and a 15% rise in adjusted EPS, compared to upgraded but less detailed guidance in the previous quarter.
  • There is a focus on structural change in working capital and supply chain management, while continuing margin and simplification initiatives.
  • Analysts’ questions shifted from growth drivers and margin expansion in Q3 to a deeper focus on cash flow mechanics, working capital, and tariff impacts in Q4.
  • Key metrics such as backlog, margin improvement, and cash flow conversion targets showed sequential progress with more robust forward-looking commitments.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted ongoing tariff pressures, particularly in Commercial Aircraft, which are expected to continue impacting margins.
  • Working capital and receivables timing are risks to free cash flow conversion, particularly with higher sales and back-loaded earnings.
  • Capital expenditures remain elevated to support growth and operational improvements.
  • Some customer advances were pulled forward into FY '25, potentially reducing their impact in FY '26.

Final Takeaway

Moog management emphasized record-setting results for both the fourth quarter and fiscal year, citing strong performance across Defense, Aerospace, and Industrial segments. With a $3 billion backlog and robust cash flow, the company projects 9% sales growth and a 15% EPS increase for fiscal 2026. Management remains focused on structural improvements in working capital and supply chain, continued margin expansion, and targeted acquisitions, while actively addressing tariff and mix headwinds in Commercial Aircraft. The company expresses confidence in both its operational execution and its ability to deliver on forward guidance for the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: AI startup Sierra hits $100M ARR milestone: report
data.description:
• Artificial intelligence startup Sierra, led by former Salesforce (CRM) co-CEO Bret Taylor, has hit a milestone of $100M in annual recurring revenue, tech reporter Alex Heath said.
  • That's up from roughly $20M a year ago, Heath reported on his website. Like other enterprise software firms such as Salesforce, Sierra signs deals for at least 12 months — oftentimes multi-year deals — bills its customers annual and allows them to take up to 30 days before paying.
  • Sierra, which creates AI agents for customer experience teams and developers via its Agent OS, is led by Taylor. He is also the Chairman of OpenAI, a board member at Shopify (SHOP) and has worked at Meta Platforms (META) and other tech companies.
  • In September, Sierra announced it had raised $350M in new funding, which valued it at $10B.
  • Sierra did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: United Bankshares announces plan to repurchase up to 5M shares of common stock
data.description:
• United Bankshares (UBSI) announced a new plan to repurchase up to 5 million shares of common stock, or about 3.6% of the company’s total shares of common stock issued and outstanding.
  • From October 1, 2025 through November 20, 2025, United purchased 1,103,666 shares of its common stock under the 2022 Plan at a weighted average price of $35.99.
  • Shares of the company were up 4% at $37.34 on Friday.
  • Filing

data.title: Merck Keytruda formulations approved in U.S. for bladder cancer
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration ((FDA)) on Friday approved injectable and intravenous versions of Merck’s (MRK) blockbuster anti-PD-1 therapy Keytruda for bladder cancer as part of a combination regimen with Pfizer (PFE) and Astellas’ (OTCPK:ALPMF) (OTCPK:ALPMY) Padcev.

Accordingly, intravenous Keytruda and subcutaneously delivered Keytruda Qlex will be available in the U.S. for use in combination with Padcev, an antibody-drug conjugate, for adults with muscle-invasive bladder cancer.

The treatments are indicated for neoadjuvant treatment (before surgery) followed by adjuvant treatment (after surgery) for those who are ineligible for cisplatin-based chemotherapy, according to the FDA.

The approvals were based on the companies’ KEYNOTE-905 trial, in which Keytruda plus Padcev as perioperative treatment led to statistically significant improvements in event-free survival and overall survival compared to surgery in MIBC patients for whom cisplatin is not indicated.

In October, the agency had accepted the company’s supplemental Biologics License Applications ((sBLA)) for Keytruda and Keytruda Qlex for priority review, granting April 7, 2026, as the target action date.

data.title: Azenta outlines 3%–5% organic revenue growth and 300 bps margin expansion for 2026 amid operational overhaul
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Azenta, Inc. (AZTA) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO John P. Marotta highlighted the company's progress in refocusing the organization, simplifying operations, and embedding operational excellence through the Azenta Business System. He stated, "We've made considerable progress, but we have also recognized we have more to do, which excites us about the road ahead."
  • Marotta noted a major structural shift: "We have moved from a complex centralized model to one that empowers our operating companies with clear accountability and a greater agility."
  • The CEO emphasized financial strength: "The strength of our balance sheet with over $0.5 billion in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities gives us the financial flexibility to invest with discipline across 4 strategic levers."
  • Marotta announced 3% core growth and 310 basis points of margin expansion for the year, along with decisive steps to reshape the commercial organization and a focus on durable, profitable growth.
  • He shared forward-looking priorities: "We anticipate core revenue growth between 3% to 5%, and expected adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of 300 basis points, and higher free cash flow generation as we scale our operational improvements."
  • CFO Lawrence Lin stated, "Fourth quarter revenue was $159 million, up 6% year-over-year on a reported basis, and up 4% organically with Multiomics delivering a record quarter."
  • Lin reported, "Non-GAAP EPS for the fourth quarter was $0.21, and was $0.51 for the full year. I'm pleased to report an adjusted EBITDA margin of 13% in the fourth quarter, and 11.2% for the full year."
  • Lin highlighted liquidity: "We ended the year in a strong financial position with $546 million in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities, providing us with the flexibility to invest in growth initiatives and return value to shareholders over time."

Outlook

  • The company anticipates organic revenue growth in the range of 3% to 5% for fiscal 2026.
  • Multiomics is expected to deliver low single-digit growth, while Sample Management Solutions is expected to contribute mid-single-digit growth.
  • Lin stated, "We expect a slower start in the first half of the year and anticipate first quarter revenue to decline approximately 1% to 2% year-over-year. We expect the second half of the year to accelerate as our commercial investments and growth initiatives gain traction."
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of approximately 300 basis points is targeted for fiscal 2026, with improved free cash flow generation by over 30% year-over-year.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenue was reported at $159 million, with 6% growth year-over-year and 4% organic growth.
  • Multiomics delivered record revenue of $73 million, representing 11% growth on a reported basis and 10% organic growth, with sequencing volume rising 50% year-over-year.
  • Sample Management Solutions revenue was $86 million, up 2% reported and flat organically, while non-GAAP gross margin for SMS rose to 49.3%.
  • Non-GAAP gross margin for the quarter was 46.7%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $21 million, with a 13% margin.
  • Free cash flow usage was $6 million for the quarter, attributed to timing of revenue and project-related billing; full-year free cash flow was $38 million.

Q&A

  • David Saxon, Needham: Asked about growth guidance, macro factors, and NIH funding impact. Marotta explained, "A lot of what we're seeing is the slowdown on capital expenditures. That's continued to impact our stores in Cryo... The government shutdown from last month is really weighing on some of the guidance."
  • Saxon: Asked about SMS growth confidence. Lin responded, "For C&I, we feel really good about kind of where we are... We expect robust growth in SRS, really through two things... Our commercial leaders close two meaningful big deals."
  • Steven Etoch, Stephens: Inquired about customer base trends and Multiomics growth components. Marotta described, "We're seeing pharma. Of course, we're tapping into the profit pools of pharma and biotech right now."
  • Andrew Cooper, Raymond James: Focused on SMS segment growth and price optimization. Marotta detailed, "You're looking at contracts of 7 to 25 years, extremely stable, reoccurring revenue is in the 90% range in that part of our portfolio."
  • Vijay Kumar, Evercore: Questioned macro impact and segment assumptions for Q1. Marotta explained the government shutdown's influence and CapEx softness. Lin added, "CapEx... that's weaker from a negative growth perspective in SMS, right?"
  • Jacqueline Kisa, TD Cowen: Asked about M&A strategy. Marotta specified a focus on tuck-ins to expand SRS, automated solutions, and synthesis.
  • Paul Knight, KeyBanc: Sought views on market growth for stores and M&A focus. Marotta said, "Stores in Cryo, we think, are low single digit right now... we're still an outgrowth story based on us capturing market share."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a slightly cautious tone, probing for specifics on macro risks, segment performance, and guidance confidence, especially regarding the impact of government funding and CapEx slowdowns.
  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined stance, frequently emphasizing operational improvements, investment in growth, and resilience. Marotta stated, "We are a stronger company today operationally, culturally and strategically. And we are confident in the path ahead."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' questions showed increased focus on near-term macro pressures, while management's tone became more assertive about structural changes and growth strategy despite persistent market headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 maintains a similar 3%–5% organic revenue growth range, but the company now expects a slower start in the first half of the year and a stronger acceleration in the second half.
  • Margin expansion targets increased, with 300 basis points of adjusted EBITDA margin expansion for 2026 compared to reaffirming the same in Q3.
  • Management further streamlined the organization, shifting to a decentralized model with greater regional accountability and more direct customer alignment.
  • Analysts in the current call placed heightened emphasis on macro uncertainty, government funding delays, and execution risk, while management provided greater detail on operational levers and execution progress than in the previous quarter.
  • The commercial leadership and regional go-to-market initiatives, discussed as new or developing in Q3, are now described as deeply embedded and producing measurable results.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty, particularly around capital spending and the impact of the recent government shutdown.
  • CapEx slowdown and delayed customer decisions remain key headwinds, especially affecting Cryogenic Stores and Automated Storage businesses.
  • Management is actively monitoring the macro environment and has structured new commercial and pricing initiatives to mitigate these risks.
  • Government funding delays, particularly from NIH, contributed to booking softness and are expected to push some revenue into future quarters.

Final Takeaway

Azenta's leadership underscored its transformation into a more agile, accountable, and customer-focused organization, supported by a robust balance sheet and operational discipline. While macro uncertainty and capital spending delays present headwinds, the company projects 3%–5% organic revenue growth and 300 basis points of margin expansion for fiscal 2026, with expectations for stronger performance in the second half. Strategic investments in commercial capabilities, regional leadership, and core business tuck-in acquisitions are central to driving long-term value creation and profitable growth.
